reason: TM misreads package references as variables (red) / import paths as strings (green); namespace yellow is the correct meaning and matches ODP's namespace color
consts are orange by design (constants merged into the literal family, ecosystem-majority ruling 2026-07-20); the Go TM grammar cannot see constness
const locals are orange by design (constants merged into the literal family, 2026-07-20); TM only marks some declaration sites
enum members are compile-time constants - orange like variable.readonly (constant/literal merge 2026-07-20); TM has no enum-member awareness
constructor calls: TM guesses function-call blue; semantic class yellow keeps the type family consistent (approved look since Record<> screenshot)
ODP paints object receivers (variable.other.object) yellow; semantic cannot opt out per-language, and uniform red has been the shipped look since v0.0.2
same as variable: parameters used as object receivers get TM receiver-yellow
mid-chain properties (a.b.c) get ODP receiver-yellow via variable.other.object.property; same decision as variable receivers: identity-consistent red wins
